---
title: "Reproducibilitea 2026-07-22"
date: "2026-06-23"
categories: [reproducibilitea, reproducibility, sheffield]
image: ./accessible-learning-paths.jpg
from: markdown+emoji
toc: true
toc-depth: 4
toc-location: right
execute:
  code_fold: true
  code_link: true
  code_tools: true
  fig-cap-location: top
  tbl-cap-location: top
  warning: false
---

![The Turing Way project illustration by Scriberia. Used under a CC-BY 4.0 licence. DOI:
[10.5281/zenodo.3332807](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3332807)](./accessible-learning-paths.jpg)

## What

The chosen article that we will review and discuss is...

- [Petrescu, D. (2005). Losing control, keeping desire. In P. Blundell Jones, D. Petrescu, & J. Till (Eds.),
  Architecture and participation (pp. 43–64). London: Spon  Press](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/240917065_Losing_control_keeping_desire?enrichId=rgreq-e99e3935932ea6590ba4b7be1d69dc92-XXX&enrichSource=Y292ZXJQYWdlOzI0MDkxNzA2NTtBUzo0MjI2NDMxNjY5MTI1MTZAMTQ3Nzc3NzM5MTgyOQ%3D%3D&el=1_x_3)


> In Losing Control, Keeping Desire, Doina Petrescu explores how meaningful participation emerges when experts
> relinquish some control and create space for collective agency and shared decision-making. This discussion offers an
> interesting lens through which to examine open research practices, highlighting themes of collaboration,
> co-production, transparency, and shared ownership of knowledge. The session will consider how these ideas relate to
> reproducibility, participatory research, citizen science, and the broader movement towards more open and inclusive
> research cultures.


[Domi Smithson](https://profiles.shu.ac.uk/9674-domi-smithson) will take us through this paper highlighting some salient points and opening discussion.

## Where

This event will be hybrid, if you wish to attend in person we will be meeting in Room 04 06A (Level 4) of the [Langsett
Building, Sheffield Hallam University Campus](https://www.openstreetmap.org/way/1329419929).

Add the event to your Google Calendar using this
[link](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=TEMPLATE&tmeid=MWM4cGJ1Y2xzdWc1bTV0NjRiNTgxNTlhcWQgbi5zaGVwaGFyZEBzaGVmZmllbGQuYWMudWs&tmsrc=n.shephard%40sheffield.ac.uk).

If you aren't able to attend in person but would like to join the Journal Club you can do so using [Google
Meet](https://meet.google.com/jbb-xxtd-cmj).

## When

2026-07-22 @ 12:00-13:00

## Future Events

To stay abreast of up-coming events you can either sign up to the [mailing
list](https://groups.google.com/a/sheffield.ac.uk/g/reproducibilitea) or subscribe to the [RSS
feed](https://reproducibilitea-sheffield.github.io/index.xml).
